# Memory Audit — Sanctum vs jcode / Rufus / Obsidian / Understand-Anything

**Operator verbatim 2026-05-24T20:19Z:** *"do a complete audit of our memory and compare it to the full audit of rufus, understand anything, ubsidan, and most importantly JCODE"*

**Scope:** Sanctum lane research subagent brief. No code changes — analysis only.

## 1. Our memory primitives inventory

| Primitive | Role | Persistence | Recall mechanism | Size today |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `_shared-memory/knowledge/*.md` + `_INDEX.md` | Doctrine "brain" | Markdown on disk + git | Manual grep + INDEX scan on cold-start | 166 row table, 173-line index, ~160 doctrine files |
| `_shared-memory/PROGRESS/<lane>.md` | Per-lane append-only event log | Markdown on disk + git | Tail-read on session restart | 32 lane files |
| `_shared-memory/resume-points/<display>/<utc>.json` | "Like never closed" spawn state | JSON on disk + git | Latest-by-mtime per lane on cold-start | 20 lane dirs |
| `_shared-memory/operator-utterances.jsonl` | Every operator message + ack state | JSONL append-only | Tail 10 where status=new\|acknowledged | 103 rows |
| `_shared-memory/counter-arguments.jsonl` | Contradiction system | JSONL | Manual grep | 6 rows |
| `_shared-memory/improvement-log.jsonl` | Forever-improve findings | JSONL | `forever-improve.ps1 -Action Tally` | 18 rows |
| `_shared-memory/fleet-updates.jsonl` | Broadcast channel | JSONL | `fleet-update.ps1 -Action List` poll | 12 rows |
| `_shared-memory/heartbeats/*.json` | Agent liveness | JSON | Stat + parse on demand | 30+ slugs |
| `_shared-memory/cross-agent/` + `inbox/<slug>/` | Inter-agent messaging | Files / JSON | `sinister-bus.inbox_poll` MCP | active |
| `forge-memory` CLI (`_shared-memory/forge-memory/_index.json`) | TF-IDF disk recall | JSON inverted index | `forge-memory recall <q>` | live |
| Ruflo MCP `memory_*` (50+ tools) | HNSW + semantic memory bridge | External daemon storage | `memory_search`, `memory_retrieve`, `hierarchical-recall` | wired (deferred tools) |

**LOC ballpark** (our memory plumbing, excluding doctrines themselves): forge-memory ~400 LOC PowerShell+JSON, sinister-bus heartbeat/inbox ~300 LOC, forever-improve.ps1 ~600 LOC, fleet-update.ps1 ~200 LOC, log-/ack-operator-utterance.ps1 ~150 LOC each. **Total ~2000 LOC of memory glue.**

## 2. Per-system comparison

| Dimension | **Sanctum (us)** | **jcode 0.12.4** | **Rufus / Anthropic** | **Obsidian** | **Understand-Anything** |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Storage substrate | Markdown + JSONL + JSON on disk + git | Single graph file (memories+tags+clusters+edges in one HashMap struct, see `crates/jcode-memory-types/src/graph.rs:231`) | Filesystem of files (memory tool `memory_20250818`: view/create/str_replace/insert/delete/rename); CLAUDE.md hierarchy | Vault = folder of .md files | `.understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json` (one file) |
| Recall method | Grep + TF-IDF (forge-memory) + HNSW (Ruflo when called) | ONNX all-MiniLM-L6-v2 384-dim cosine + skill bonus + `top_k_by_score` | Filesystem grep + sub-agent Markdown summarizer ("Auto Memory") + "Auto Dream" reorg pass | `[[wikilink]]` backlinks + force-directed graph view | Multi-agent pipeline output queried via dashboard / chat skill |
| Verify-before-inject | NO (raw recall surfaced) | YES — Haiku sidecar grades 30 candidates → keeps top 10 (`MEMORY_RELEVANCE_MAX_CANDIDATES=30`, `MAX_RESULTS=10`, `memory.rs:58-59`) | YES — sub-agent compaction at 10k tokens + Auto Dream culls stale | NO (manual) | YES at build-time (file-analyzer agent summarizes), NO at query-time |
| Graph edges | NO (flat doctrines linked by prose "composes with") | YES — `EdgeKind { HasTag, InCluster, RelatesTo{weight}, Supersedes, ClusterEntry }` + `GRAPH_VERSION` migrations | NO (flat files) | YES — wikilinks form implicit edges | YES — file→function→class→dep edges |
| Cross-session continuity | YES — resume-points + PROGRESS + utterances | YES — `MemoryAgent` runs alongside main, channel-fed context updates, surfaces ≤5 mems/turn, resets every 50 turns | YES — memory tool persists across sessions; Managed Agents public-beta Apr 2026 | YES (it's a filesystem) | Build-time only; no per-session deltas |
| Cost per recall | $0 (local grep/TF-IDF) OR ~1 embedding call (Ruflo) | ~1 embedding (cached LRU 128) + 1 Haiku verify call per turn | 1 sub-agent call per 5k new tokens + compaction at 10k | $0 | $0 at query (built once) |
| Active maintenance | Manual (operator + forever-improve.ps1) | Automatic — cluster refinement every 50 maintenance ticks; staleness check; reinforcement on hit | Auto Dream cycle (background) + compaction | Manual | None (rebuild on demand) |
| What it's optimized for | Multi-agent fleet coordination + operator-action queue + doctrine durability | Single-agent code session with cross-project carryover | Single-agent / managed-agent with token savings (84% per Anthropic bench) | Human PKM | Code understanding/onboarding |

## 3. Three gaps in our system that the others fill

1. **No verify-before-inject loop.** When we recall doctrines we dump raw matches into context. jcode's `memory_agent.rs` runs every retrieval through a Haiku grader that separates `verified_ids` from `rejected_ids` (see `RetrievalContext` struct, `memory_agent.rs:29-36`). Rufus / Claude memory tool does this via sub-agent compaction. **Cost:** we burn context tokens on irrelevant doctrines; agents have to re-grep when matches are noisy.

2. **No typed edges between memories.** Our brain links doctrines only by prose ("composes with: X, Y, Z"). jcode encodes `HasTag / InCluster / RelatesTo{weight} / Supersedes` as first-class graph edges with a `GRAPH_VERSION` for migrations. Obsidian gets typed-edge behaviour from `[[wikilinks]]` + Dataview. **Cost:** when we deprecate a doctrine (e.g. archived `exe-dll-crash-incomplete-copy.md`) nothing automatically flags the doctrines that referenced it.

3. **No background maintenance / "Auto Dream" pass.** jcode runs cluster refinement every 50 maintenance ticks and reset-surface every 50 turns. Rufus has Auto Dream. We have `forever-improve.ps1` but it's manually triggered per work-unit and reviews CHANGES, not the brain corpus itself. **Cost:** brain has 166 rows trending toward the rule-8 soft cap of 150; nothing automatically clusters near-duplicate doctrines or supersedes stale ones (we just archived 4 today, all manual).

## 4. Three things WE do that NONE of the others do

1. **Operator-utterance tracking with ack-lifecycle.** `operator-utterances.jsonl` (103 rows) captures every verbatim operator message with `status: new|acknowledged|resolved` + tags. NONE of jcode/Rufus/Obsidian/Understand-Anything separate operator intent from agent transcript — they treat all messages as conversation. Ours is durable across sessions and surfaces unresolved items on cold-start (step 8).
2. **Per-lane PROGRESS append-only logs separate from main transcript.** 32 lane files isolate "what shipped" from "what was discussed", queryable by lane. jcode has one memory store per project_dir; no lane concept. Rufus/managed-agents have memory-per-agent but not per-deliverable-lane within an agent.
3. **Fleet-update broadcast channel + cross-agent inbox.** `fleet-updates.jsonl` + `inbox/<slug>/` enable multi-agent coordination with priority routing (high surfaces immediately, normal at end-of-turn, low silent-ack). jcode and Rufus are single-agent; Obsidian is single-human; Understand-Anything is single-pass build. We're the only one shipping multi-agent memory mesh.

## 5. Top 3 cherry-pickable improvements (ROI-ranked)

| Rank | Improvement | Source | What to lift | Effort | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Verify-before-inject Haiku grader** on brain recall (D2 from jcode-port-plan, already drafted) | jcode `memory_agent.rs` `RetrievalContext` + Anthropic memory tool pattern | ~50 LOC Python wrapper around `mcp__ruflo__memory_search` → call Haiku to grade top-N candidates → return filtered. Wire into cold-start step 6 (brain grep). | 1 lane-iter | LOW — fail-open (if Haiku unavailable, return raw matches) |
| **2** | **Typed `Supersedes` edges** in `_INDEX.md` rows | jcode `EdgeKind::Supersedes` + Obsidian wikilink | Add `supersedes:` + `superseded_by:` columns to `_INDEX.md` schema; archive script auto-sets both sides. Today we just delete (4 today) — lossy. | 1 lane-iter | LOW — additive metadata, INDEX still readable |
| **3** | **Auto-cluster / dedupe pass** ("dream cycle") over brain when row count >150 | jcode `CLUSTER_REFINEMENT_INTERVAL=50` + Rufus Auto Dream | Compose `forever-improve.ps1 -Action Drain` with new `-Action Consolidate` mode: embed all 166 rows, cluster, surface dedupe candidates to operator queue. Fires when rule-8 signal trips. | 2 lane-iters | MEDIUM — needs Ruflo embeddings up; operator must approve merges (no auto-delete) |

Not on list (intentionally deferred): full ONNX in-process embedder (we'd pay disk + dependency cost for ~0 gain over Ruflo MCP). Obsidian-style `[[wikilink]]` rewrite of brain corpus (huge churn, low payoff vs Supersedes edges).

## 6. Recommendation

**Deepen the existing Ruflo MCP wrapper (D2 from `jcode-port-plan-eve-2026-05-24`) — do not pivot architecture.** Our substrate (markdown + JSONL + git + Ruflo HNSW bridge) is correct and operator-aligned (transparent, version-controlled, lane-scoped). The three highest-ROI fixes (verify-grader, Supersedes edges, dedupe pass) are all ~50-200 LOC additions that compose with what we have — none require schema migration. Pivoting to a jcode-style monolithic `MemoryGraph` HashMap or an Obsidian-style wikilink rewrite would burn weeks for marginal gain and lose our multi-agent / multi-lane / operator-utterance strengths that none of the comparison systems have. Sequence: D2 verify-grader this iter → Supersedes edges next iter → dream-cycle when brain count crosses 150 (currently 166 in INDEX, so trigger is live — schedule for next consolidation turn).

## Composes with

- `jcode-port-plan-eve-2026-05-24.md` — this audit validates and prioritizes D2.
- `no-bullshit-tested-before-claimed-doctrine-2026-05-23.md` — rule 8 quality-degradation signal "brain >150 rows" is live; supports recommendation #3.
- `forever-improve-review-doctrine-2026-05-24.md` — extend with `-Action Consolidate` per recommendation #3.
- `safe-quality-loops-doctrine-2026-05-24.md` — the ONNX/Haiku verify pattern referenced there comes from jcode (this audit confirms the pattern + sizes the port).

## Sources

- jcode-0.12.4 source: `C:\Users\Zonia\Desktop\jcode-0.12.4\src\memory.rs` (1820 lines), `memory_agent.rs` (1706), `embedding.rs` (493), `crates/jcode-memory-types/src/graph.rs` (defines EdgeKind / MemoryGraph / TagEntry / ClusterEntry / GraphMetadata).
- Anthropic memory tool: platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/memory-tool (type: memory_20250818, 6 ops).
- Claude Code three-layer leak (Apr 2026): persistent memory.md + grep + Chyros daemon; Auto Memory + Auto Dream pattern.
- Obsidian: vault = .md folder; `[[wikilink]]` backlinks form implicit graph; Canvas + 3D-Graph plugins for visualization.
- Understand-Anything (Lum1104): multi-agent pipeline (Project Scanner → File Analyzer → Tour Builder) → `.understand-anything/knowledge-graph.json`.
